growth_records.cite: Hunt et al. Electromagnetic Biostimulation of Living Cultures for Biotechnology, Biofuel and Bioenergy Applications. Int. J. Mol. Sci. (2009). DOI:10.3390/ijms10104515. [PMC2790121]
growth_records.method: reported (specific growth rate) / doubling time calculated
growth_records.snippet: Hirano et al. [26] reported a significantly higher specific growth rate of 0.22 d-1 in S. platensis exposed to 10 mT magnetic field when compared to 0.14 d-1 for untreated culture.
growth_records.notes: This is a REVIEW article; the rate is cited from primary source Hirano et al., 'Magnetic field effects on photosynthesis and growth of the cyanobacterium spirulina platensis', J. Ferment. Bioeng., 1998, vol. 86 (ref [26]). UNTREATED/control condition. Reported specific growth rate = 0.14 d-1. Converted to per-h: 0.14/24 = 0.005833 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.14 = 4.95 d = 4.95*24 = 118.8 h. No medium composition, temperature, pH, or uptake/secretion rates given in the review. Arthrospira/Spirulina platensis is a cyanobacterium (prokaryote).

growth_records.cite: Hunt et al. Electromagnetic Biostimulation of Living Cultures for Biotechnology, Biofuel and Bioenergy Applications. Int. J. Mol. Sci. (2009). DOI:10.3390/ijms10104515. [PMC2790121]
growth_records.method: reported (specific growth rate) / doubling time calculated
growth_records.snippet: Hirano et al. [26] reported a significantly higher specific growth rate of 0.22 d-1 in S. platensis exposed to 10 mT magnetic field when compared to 0.14 d-1 for untreated culture.
growth_records.notes: REVIEW article; rate cited from primary source Hirano et al. 1998, J. Ferment. Bioeng. vol. 86 (ref [26]). MAGNETIC-FIELD-TREATED condition (10 mT). Reported specific growth rate = 0.22 d-1. Converted to per-h: 0.22/24 = 0.009167 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.22 = 3.15 d = 3.15*24 = 75.6 h. No medium composition, temperature, pH, or uptake/secretion rates given. Prokaryote (cyanobacterium). Rate not measured in this paper; a perturbation (magnetic field) condition.
